docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:10:### H1. Bandits never attack — Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551` (`_transitionBanditToAttacking`) is **only called from test harnesses** (`Bandit.t.sol:18`, `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`, `BanditSpawn.t.sol`). The heartbeat steps 4–6 (`_advanceBanditStates`, `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_evaluateBanditSpawns`, `ClanWorld.sol:2243-2252`) only handle Spawned→Camped, Resting→Camped, attack-resolution-of-already-Attacking, and new spawns. There is no target-selection + state-transition step for Camped bandits, so on the live engine bandits spawn, transition to Camped, and remain there forever. Consequently `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_resolveBanditAttack`, `_distributeBanditLootToDefendingClans`, `BlueprintEarned`, `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget`, `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget`, and the `BanditTargetDied` event are **dead code in the live heartbeat path**. The PR description claims "Pending sub-issues: 9.4 Deterministic attack resolution," but the merged commit `d02061f feat(contracts): deterministic bandit attack resolution (#207)` is part of this PR — so the gap is dispatch wiring, not resolution logic. **Suggested fix:** add a step between `_advanceBanditStates` and `_resolveAttackingBandits` that, for each Camped bandit whose camp window has elapsed, picks a target via `_banditSpawnRegionWeights`-style scoring (or the loot-value heuristic in `_lootValueRaw`) and calls `_transitionBanditToAttacking`. Alternatively, file a follow-up issue and add a top-of-file comment in the heartbeat docstring explicitly marking attack dispatch as deferred so reviewers and the Convex indexer don't assume the system is live.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:15:### M1. `getDerivedClanState` / `getClanFullView` / `quoteLootValueSettled` now return "phantom" simulated state without firing events
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:28:`ClanWorld.sol:~1772-1788`: `wallDamage = remainingDamage < WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL ? remainingDamage : WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL;` followed by `if (wallDamage >= WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL) clan.wallLevel--`. So a level-5 wall absorbs the same 100 damage as a level-1 wall before being chipped, and any single attack that lands ≥100 damage chips one level. Higher-level walls give no extra incoming-damage cushion per attack — they only give *more attacks* before the wall is fully gone. Verify this matches the v4.5 spec; if the intent was "level N wall absorbs N×100", the math is off.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:30:### M6. `_resolveBanditAttack` re-settles target & defenders that step 3 already settled
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:31:`ClanWorld.sol:~1716-1717` calls `_settleClan(targetClanId)` and `_eagerSettleActiveDefendersForBase(targetClanId, …)` on every attack resolution, but step 3 (`_eagerSettleForBandits`) already settles candidate-region bases and defenders on the same closedTick. With the typical 4-clansman x 12-clan limit this is bounded, but it's pure wasted gas under the live-attack path. Once H1 is fixed and attacks actually fire, this redundancy will compound. Consider removing the redundant settle inside `_resolveBanditAttack` — the heartbeat-level eager-settle already guarantees the target is current.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:73:7. **Test coverage for the integration seam is thin.** `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol` exercises attack resolution but only via the `BanditAttackHarness.forceAttackingBandit` test hook. There is no end-to-end test that proves the full `heartbeat → spawn → camp → attack → resolve` cycle works through *normal* heartbeats — exactly because H1 makes such a test impossible today. Once H1 is fixed, add at least one integration test that drives the cycle solely through `heartbeat()` calls.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:75:Want me to `/schedule` an agent in 1 week to verify the H1 follow-up issue (Camped→Attacking dispatch) ships before Submission 1 cutoff?
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:39:### M1: `getClanFullView` — `thisClanDefendingBaseId` stores region ID, not clan ID
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:72:    if (clan.wallLevel > 0) {
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:73:        clan.wallLevel--;
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:82:The test suite covers individual components well but lacks an end-to-end test that exercises the full cycle within heartbeat: spawn → advance → camp → target selection → attack → resolve, all driven by `heartbeat()` calls without harness bypasses. The `forceAttackingBandit()` harness skips `tickEnteredState` for the Attacking state, which could mask timing bugs.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:116:2. **`_resolveAttackingBandits` array iteration is correct.** Uses `while (i < length)` with `i++` only when the bandit is NOT deleted (stays at same index if deleted, because swap-pop fills the slot). This is the correct pattern for iterate-and-delete on a dynamic array.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:118:3. **`_markClanDead` cascade design is sound.** The `excludedBanditId` parameter prevents `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget` from aborting the bandit that *caused* the death (since it's already being resolved). This prevents re-entrancy into the bandit state machine during attack resolution.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:469:`ClanWorld.sol` in `_advanceBanditStates()` and `_resolveBanditAttack()`
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:470:Although sub-issues 9.1 and 9.4 outline a complete state machine, the actual automated transitions driving it are missing. There is no logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to transition a bandit from `Camped` to `Attacking` (Target Selection is completely unimplemented). Furthermore, when an attack resolves without a defeat and a bandit transitions to `Escaped`, there is no logic to transition it back to `Resting`. As a result, once spawned, a bandit will get permanently stuck in `Camped` (or, if forcefully triggered, stuck in `Escaped`).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:471:*Suggested fix:* Implement target selection logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to evaluate eligible `Camped` bandits, pick a valid target, and call `_transitionBanditToAttacking()`. Additionally, add a transition from `Escaped` to `Resting` either immediately during attack resolution or as an automated step in `_advanceBanditStates()`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:483:*Suggested fix:* Update the logic to allow the wall to absorb up to `clan.wallLevel * WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` total damage. Calculate the number of wall levels lost based on the actual damage absorbed, and deduct multiple levels if the damage exceeds a single level's HP threshold.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:492:**BanditAttackResolved event hardcodes stolen resources to zero**
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:493:`ClanWorld.sol` in `_resolveBanditAttack()`
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:494:The `BanditAttackResolved` event emits hardcoded `0`s for the `stolenWood`, `stolenIron`, `stolenWheat`, and `stolenFish` fields even if the bandit escapes successfully. If resource stealing is deferred to a future phase, it should be documented as a stub; otherwise, the bandit should actually drain resources from the target clan's vault upon an un-defeated attack.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:503:The tests `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol` and `Bandit.t.sol` manually expose and invoke `_transitionBanditToAttacking()` via their harness contracts. By forcefully mocking the transition into the `Attacking` state, the tests pass perfectly and obscure the fact that the underlying state machine lacks the autonomous target selection logic required for the feature to function in production. Avoid mocking control flow that the protocol itself is responsible for orchestrating.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:25:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:1717` | 5.5 = **1/5** | HIGH | **`_resolveBanditAttack` reverts on target-death-during-attack.** `_settleClan(targetClanId)` may kill the target → `_markClanDead` aborts the attacking bandit → state machine then tries to transition already-`Escaped` bandit again at `:1758`, which is invalid. Race window. Fix: re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target/defender settlement; return if cleanup already handled. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:43:| L2 | `BanditAttackResolved` event hardcodes stolen resources to 0 (resource theft deferred?). Document or implement. | Gemini |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:46:| L5 | `_resolveBanditAttack` re-settles target/defenders that step 3 already settled. Wasted gas under live-attack path (compounds when H1 fixes). | 4.7 |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:56:3. **Test harness mocks mask the H1 gap.** `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol` and `Bandit.t.sol` use `forceAttackingBandit()` harness helpers. The integration seam is untested at the heartbeat-driven cycle level. Once H1 is fixed, add an end-to-end heartbeat test.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:65:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS_FIXES — 4 HIGH (Camped→Attacking + 2 ABI + revert race)
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:67:- **Opus 4.7:** NEEDS_FIXES — 1 HIGH (Camped→Attacking) + 7 MED + 7 LOW
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:68:- **Gemini 3.1 Pro:** NEEDS_FIXES — 2 HIGH (Camped→Attacking + eager-settle skips with cap) + 2 MED + 2 LOW (succeeded after retry)
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:72:- Findings flagged by 4 models: **1** (Camped→Attacking — the most critical)
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:21:1. Camped→Attacking dispatch in heartbeat — does production now actually drive the bandit attack lifecycle? Test it.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:25:5. _resolveBanditAttack revert race — does it now safely return when settlement kills target mid-attack?

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3911:+        target.forceAttackingBandit(target.getClan(firstClanId).baseRegion, 100, firstClanId);
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3912:+        target.forceAttackingBandit(target.getClan(secondClanId).baseRegion, 100, secondClanId);

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8303:  The ABI regression tests only prove `getWorldState()` and `getWorldSnapshot()` decoding. `IChainClient.getClanFullView()` now also depends on the generated ABI, but there is no decode fixture covering that deeper nested tuple.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8304:- `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:483`  
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8305:  The target-death race test proves the final state no longer reverts and ends in `Escaped`, but it does not assert that `BanditAttackResolved` was not emitted after the target died. A future double-processing regression could still pass this test.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8311:  - Heartbeat wiring is live via `heartbeat() -> _advanceBanditStates() -> _resolveAttackingBandits()`.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8315:  - `_resolveBanditAttack()` now safely returns if settlement kills the target mid-attack.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8339:  The ABI regression tests only prove `getWorldState()` and `getWorldSnapshot()` decoding. `IChainClient.getClanFullView()` now also depends on the generated ABI, but there is no decode fixture covering that deeper nested tuple.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8340:- `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:483`  
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8341:  The target-death race test proves the final state no longer reverts and ends in `Escaped`, but it does not assert that `BanditAttackResolved` was not emitted after the target died. A future double-processing regression could still pass this test.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8347:  - Heartbeat wiring is live via `heartbeat() -> _advanceBanditStates() -> _resolveAttackingBandits()`.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8351:  - `_resolveBanditAttack()` now safely returns if settlement kills the target mid-attack.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14955:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14964:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1709-1718, 1948-1950, 518-546` — `_resolveBanditAttack()` checks `targetClanState`, then settles the target, but never re-checks whether that settlement killed the clan. If the target dies during pre-attack settlement, the current attacker still resolves a normal attack path against an already-dead target, while only the other attackers get `BanditTargetDied`. Suggested fix: re-check target liveness immediately after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` and escape/abort the current attacker too.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14968:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1177-1182, 3402-3446` — Derived simulation does not mirror live defender bookkeeping. A simulated `DefendBase` arrival never registers a defender, and simulated death paths do not release defender registry state, yet `getClanFullView()` mixes simulated clan data with raw defender indexes. That can make derived views disagree with what real settlement would show. Suggested fix: either simulate defender-registry side effects too, or stop composing simulated state with raw defender indexes.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14997:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15006:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1709-1718, 1948-1950, 518-546` — `_resolveBanditAttack()` checks `targetClanState`, then settles the target, but never re-checks whether that settlement killed the clan. If the target dies during pre-attack settlement, the current attacker still resolves a normal attack path against an already-dead target, while only the other attackers get `BanditTargetDied`. Suggested fix: re-check target liveness immediately after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` and escape/abort the current attacker too.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15010:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1177-1182, 3402-3446` — Derived simulation does not mirror live defender bookkeeping. A simulated `DefendBase` arrival never registers a defender, and simulated death paths do not release defender registry state, yet `getClanFullView()` mixes simulated clan data with raw defender indexes. That can make derived views disagree with what real settlement would show. Suggested fix: either simulate defender-registry side effects too, or stop composing simulated state with raw defender indexes.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:17:Senior staff engineer FINAL pre-merge review for PR #199 (Phase 8 — Buildings + Progression) at head 33193b7.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 8 ships: wall upgrades (8.1) + base upgrades (8.2) + monument upgrades w/ blueprint gating L7-10 (8.3) + score / rank getters (8.4). Builds on Phase 5 vault + Phase 9 blueprint reward.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2192:Reviewing PR #199 at `33193b7` as a final pre-merge pass. I’m starting by loading the contracts package guidance and the surrounding contract/test context, then I’ll split the sweep across subagents so we can cover upgrades, rankings, and interaction risks in parallel.

docs/guides/stream-frontend.md:38:(Wave 1+) An `RegionSvg` component imports the SVG inline and overlays clan-color fills based on `WorldSnapshot.regions[].ownerClanId`. SVG keeps the cut-list shallow — if Pixi work is behind, we can ship region polygons as plain SVG without a canvas at all.
docs/guides/stream-frontend.md:42:Pixi is for the canvas only — animated elements, sprite-based units, particle effects. The UI chrome (panels, modals, whisper feed) stays in plain React. The Pixi canvas is a sibling element in the `<App>` tree, not the root.
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:22:make agent-fund       # send 0.1 ETH from faucet to each agent wallet
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:35:5. Re-fund agent wallets from faucet.
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:47:Agent wallets are hot-keys. Cap each at 0.5 ETH testnet. Refund as needed.
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:75:Two-wallet model for S2:
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:76:- **Treasury wallet:** offline-signed, holds the multisig that owns the engine contract.
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:77:- **Agent wallets:** hot, capped, replenished from faucet.
docs/guides/stream-ops.md:79:Never mix the two. Treasury wallet keys never exist on disk on the demo machine.
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:34:| 2 | `runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts` | 51–56 | **Runner heartbeat targets World Chain Sepolia (chainId 4801) instead of Base Sepolia (84532).** After the Base Sepolia chain pivot (PR #125), `RealChainClient`, `foundry.toml`, `start-heartbeat-loop.sh`, and `deployments/base-sepolia.json` all target chainId 84532. But `RunnerCastHeartbeat` still defines `worldChainSepolia` with `id: 4801`. Signed transactions carry wrong chain ID — they will fail, target the wrong network, or suffer replay-protection mismatch. Wave 2 confirmed this is stale config, not intentional. | Correctness |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:70:| 28 | `agents/src/__tests__/cli.test.ts` + `agents/test/cli.test.ts` | — | **Overlapping CLI test suites.** Two files with heavy overlap; different stubs/assertions. Double CI cost. Not exact duplicates — complementary but redundant. | Tests |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:94:| 47 | `runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts` 131–154 | Rate-limit guard scope | Correctly scoped: only `ContractFunctionRevertedError` upgraded; pre-flight errors pass through. |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:137:Agent 1.6 flagged a chain config split. Agent 2.1 and 2.3 both independently confirmed: `runnerCastHeartbeat.ts` defines `worldChainSepolia` with `id: 4801`, while every other component (IChainClient, foundry.toml, deployments/base-sepolia.json, start-heartbeat-loop.sh) targets Base Sepolia (`id: 84532`). This is stale configuration from before the Base Sepolia pivot (PR #125). Signed transactions carry wrong chain ID and will fail or target the wrong network.
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:184:- Consider importing `baseSepolia` from viem/chains instead of manual `defineChain`
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:42:| 5 | `runner/src/zeroGMemoryStore.ts` | 315–341 | **`OG_STORAGE_API_KEY` is a feature flag, not an API credential.** It is never passed to `buildRealBatcherFactory` or any SDK call — only its presence gates 0G mode. `README.md` calls it "0G API key", misleading operators about the security posture. Real auth depends on `ELDER_MNEMONIC`-derived wallets. Rename to `OG_STORAGE_ENABLED` or document honestly. | Security / Docs |
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:64:| 22 | (commit history) | — | **Several commits missing `(#N)` issue refs.** Affects: `feat(runner): add ClanWorld runner daemon prototype`, `fix(runner): fix-round PR #93`, `feat(runner): Phase 7/8`, `fix(runner): narrow heartbeat error upgrade`. | Conventions |
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:87:| 40 | `runner/src/axlPeerInbox.ts` | 338–343 | `peerToClan` Map rebuilt per drain | Minor CPU cost; not hot path. |
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:103:| 1.5 | Test Coverage | HIGH tickLoop untested (#20); MEDIUM tautological wallet test; MEDIUM misleading test names |
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:112:| 2.2 | 0G SDK Integration | CONFIRMED #5, #24, #25; wallet derivation is correct; cache coherency is maintained |
docs/reviews/pr136-review-claude-opus.md:169:The code quality is generally high — zero `any` usage, good abort signal threading, thoughtful adapter pattern, comprehensive tests for most modules. The 4 fix rounds (r3–r6) on the 0G adapter show healthy iteration. The main gaps are in the tick loop orchestration (the CRITICAL #1), the file-based peer inbox (MUST FIX #2 and #3), and documentation keeping pace with architecture changes.

docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:14:- region-based movement instead of fine-grained tile pathfinding
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:97:## 1.8 Homebase placement
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:98:Each clan has one homebase assigned to a valid spawn region.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:177:For a mission with travel duration `d`:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:183:If a mission is submitted during tick `307` with travel duration `1`:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:213:- goto Homebase, deposit resources
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:238:The cooldown is **time-based**, not tick-count based.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:263:- `deposit_resources` requested at a non-homebase destination
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:265:- `defend_base` references nonexistent base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:275:- arrives to defend a base that no longer exists
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:284:- deposit resources, then wait at Homebase
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:306:- defend base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:314:- build wall
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:315:- upgrade base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:316:- upgrade monument
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:325:## 3.11 `defend_base` persistence
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:326:`defend_base` is a **persistent continuous mission**.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:333:A clansman on `defend_base` remains on that mission until:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:398:Each homebase has an effectively infinite vault.

docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:406:- monument upgrades
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417:- base yield = `2e18` wood per action tick
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:423:- base yield = `0.5e18` iron per action tick
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:459:- regrow duration is `4` ticks
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:471:These costs apply only if the clan has living clansmen.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:492:- do not force heartbeat-based full clan settlement solely to flip starvation state
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:572:If multiple scheduled market actions mature in the same tick, Keeper executes them in deterministic FIFO order based on mission commit order.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:684:- baiting or shifting target priority toward another base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:690:- identify the highest loot-value homebase in the current region
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:691:- if no base exists in the region, attack resolution is a noop
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:692:- if multiple bases are tied for the highest loot-value, including a zero-loot tie, target the base with the lowest `clanId`
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.

docs/planning/DEMO_DRIFT.md:3:Enumerates every demo/fake/stub component in the codebase. Each entry notes: what it is, why it exists, and which phase removes or replaces it.
docs/planning/DEMO_DRIFT.md:30:| `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` — `MOCK_CLANS` (line 65) | Hardcoded clan definitions (id, name, homeRegion, color, archetype). Used as the primary data source for all rendering — sprites, scoreboard, travel dots, wall levels. | Phase 4 — gate behind `VITE_CLANWORLD_DEMO_MODE`; replace with live snapshot clans |
docs/planning/DEMO_DRIFT.md:31:| `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` — `MOCK_LEVEL_BY_CLAN` / `derivedMonumentLevel` (line ~226) | Monument level derived from treasury amount (`treasury / 250e18`). Treasury is a demo-mock value; real monument level comes from `getClanFullView().clan.monumentLevel`. | Phase 4 — replace with `snapshot.clans[n].monumentLevel` |
docs/planning/DEMO_DRIFT.md:33:| `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` — `MOCK_WALL_LEVELS` (line 208) | Hardcoded wall levels per clan (indexed by `MOCK_CLANS` position). | Phase 4 — replace with `getClanFullView().clan.wallLevel` |
docs/planning/DEMO_DRIFT.md:63:| `apps/landing/src/pages/LorePage.tsx` — gameplay constants | Narrative copy contains gameplay numbers (gather durations: 3 ticks, cooldown: ~3 ticks) that contradict v4 spec (cooldown = 60s / 1 tick at 60s cadence). These are lore-facing approximations, not authoritative. See `CANONICAL_SPEC.md` rank 9. | Non-authoritative; update copy when final numbers are locked for Submission 2 |

docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:12:These are proposed additions to the v4.2 / v4.3 contract spec to support the hackathon demo UI's indexer with minimum RPC cost.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:47:**Purpose:** Single-call top-level world state, including leaderboard data for every clan.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:63:    LeaderboardEntry[] leaderboard;

docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:83:- The leaderboard does not need to be sorted; UI sorts client-side
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:100:    uint32[] incomingDefenderIds; // workers from other clans currently defending this base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:101:    uint32   thisClanDefendingBaseId; // base ID this clan's defending workers are at, or 0
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:153:- The indexer stores each tick's spot price into Convex, building a per-resource price-history series for sparkline charts in the market panel.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:227:All functions are `view`. Readers and indexer pay no gas. The only cost is bytecode size for the additional structs and functions, which should be negligible.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:241:With v1's max realm size of 8 clans, unbounded array returns are safe. If a future version grows beyond ~50 clans, add pagination to `getWorldSnapshot.leaderboard`.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:16:- World logic remains tick-based; slight real-time drift does not change rules semantics.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:34:## A3. Bandit movement duration between regions
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:55:- target selection is not based only on the eventually selected base
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:56:- target selection is computed only after all candidate bases in the current region have been eagerly settled
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:92:- if a base begins a tick with `0` wood in vault,
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:101:- carried resources do not save a base from upkeep until they are actually deposited
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:109:> **[PATCHED 2026-04-26]** The original text of this section incorrectly stated that all v1 market actions are Exact Input. `market_buy` is Exact Output, not Exact Input. The corrected text is below. Authoritative source: `clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md §8.4` and `IClanWorld.sol`.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:156:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:174:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:183:- 10 defense via `defend_base`, or
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:184:- 5 defense via `WAITING` at their own homebase
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:16:1. `clanworld_v4_spec.md` — base game mechanics
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:66:**Definition of done:** working demo recordable on a phone via World App, showing all 8 demo moments from `clanworld_v1_implementation_profile.md` §8.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:86:Per the user's confirmation: **the two realms are never both live simultaneously.** Submission 1 runs on World Chain Sepolia. After that submission ships, the same codebase is redeployed and reconfigured for Submission 2 on Base Sepolia.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:90:- **One frontend codebase**, parameterized by chain config at build/deploy time.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:120:| Frontend `tickEpoch.durationMs` (from Convex) | 20000 | 60000 | 20000 |
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:148:The code stays in the codebase per backend spec §6. It is feature-flagged off by default (`HEARTBEAT_CALLER_ENABLED=false` in `.env`). Two reasons it stays:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:210:  "chain": "base-sepolia" | "worldchain-sepolia",
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:283:- **KeeperHub workflow.** Confirm the Base Sepolia workflow per v4.5 §2 is exactly what the bounty requires; check whether they want richer integration (e.g., conditional triggers based on game state).
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:323:- §2.13 (tick duration) → resolved per §2.1 of this doc
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:332:The graph applies to **one realm at a time.** Add a note: "This graph runs once for Submission 1 (World Chain Sepolia) and again for Submission 2 (Base Sepolia). Same codebase, different chain configs."
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:369:deploy-base-sepolia:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:375:fund-agents:                     # uses CHAIN env to pick the right RPC and wallet set
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:380:Single env var (`CHAIN=worldchain-sepolia` or `CHAIN=base-sepolia`) drives all per-chain behavior.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:388:- **Two submissions, one stack, one realm at a time.** Same codebase, chain config swapped between submissions.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:435:| Wallets | One per agent, gas + game txs | **Two per clan** — agent wallet (gas, game txs, KV writes) + owner wallet (iNFT control); Submission 1 may use single wallet shortcut |
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:458:- **§4 Wallet Model** — wallet derivation from mnemonic, BIP-39 paths, funding, mapping file. Still correct.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:459:- **§8.2 Strategic alignment seeds** — Aldric/Mira/Brennan/Sora archetypes are pure prompt content. Still useful for both submissions; just paste them into CLAUDE.md instead of injecting via `system_prompt`.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:486:- Pumps situation blocks based on the same triggers as 0G battleplan §9 (waiting clansmen, new whisper, mission completion, tick advanced + 60s heartbeat)
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:489:- Reads game state from Convex; submits transactions via the Elder wallet
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:491:The single-wallet shortcut (battleplan §9) is acceptable for Submission 1.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:511:- Two-wallet model: agent wallet + owner wallet, never co-located
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:518:- Submission 2 adds: Town Square panel (3 slots × N clans, reads from 0G KV bulletins via Convex mirror), Owner Editor UI (separate route, wallet-connected, edits iNFT metadata), Transfer Demo flow (the killer Track 2 moment)
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:549:4. **The orchestrator spec** — full version with situation pump, AXL bridge, DEK reading, multi-chain event listening, two-wallet model
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:593:**What:** a wallet-connected page where the human owner edits the persistent strategy & notes tail of their clan's CLAUDE.md, signs the change with their owner wallet, and triggers an `updateMetadata()` tx on 0G iNFT contract.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:595:**Why:** the Track 2 prize narrative includes "dynamic upgrades" — owners updating their agent's intelligence post-mint. Without this UI, the dynamic-upgrade story is hand-waved. With it, judges see a real demo.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:601:**Dependencies:** ClanIdentity library (read-write mode), wallet connector, iNFT contract deployed.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:606:1. Owner of clan 7 transfers iNFT to a new wallet
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:610:5. The new Elder's first action references something specific from the prior owner's gameplay (e.g., "based on my notes, clan 3 betrayed us at tick 412, do not trust them")
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:628:- **Two submissions, one stack, one realm at a time.** Same codebase, chain config swapped between submissions.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:629:- **Submission 1 (World) is first.** World Chain Sepolia, 20s ticks, Foundry-loop heartbeat, MiniKit-wrapped frontend, simple Claude Code Elders with minimal `elder` toolbelt, single wallet per agent, Convex `whispers` table.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:630:- **Submission 2 (Base + OpenAgents Track 2 + others) is later.** Base Sepolia, 60s ticks, KeeperHub heartbeat. Plus: 0G iNFT (ERC-7857 with MockOracle) for Elder identity, 0G KV for agent memory + town square bulletins, AXL for private whispers, two-wallet model, Owner Editor UI, Transfer Demo. Plus possibly: 0G Compute sealed inference (model selection TBD).
docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 7 ships: gold transfer + vault transfer + blueprint transfer + bundled transfer + dead-clan restrict. Atomic-swap pattern (proposer + acceptor, NOT order book per Liam directive). Builds on Phase 5/6/10.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7104:  Proposals store `from`, `to`, amounts, expiry, and flags, but not the proposer address, owner at proposal time, or an owner/IFT nonce. `accept*Transfer` validates only the current target owner. If `fromClan.owner` changes after proposal creation, the target can still accept and debit the new owner’s clan. This affects gold, vault, blueprint, and bundled transfers. Store proposer/owner nonce and require it at accept, or invalidate open proposals on ownership transfer.
docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7155:  Proposals store `from`, `to`, amounts, expiry, and flags, but not the proposer address, owner at proposal time, or an owner/IFT nonce. `accept*Transfer` validates only the current target owner. If `fromClan.owner` changes after proposal creation, the target can still accept and debit the new owner’s clan. This affects gold, vault, blueprint, and bundled transfers. Store proposer/owner nonce and require it at accept, or invalidate open proposals on ownership transfer.
docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:17:- Path B (impl-correct): patch spec §8.3-8.4 to match vault-based trades. Document deviation. Zero code change. Spec amendment.
docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:23:| H1: `ClanWorld.sol:1657, 1669, 1825, 1773, 1885+` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 = **3/3** | HIGH | **Market sell/buy bypass worker carry, mutate vault directly.** Spec §8.3: sell = worker carry → gold purse. §8.4: buy = gold purse → worker carry. Impl: sells `_deductFromVault`, buys `_addToVault`. BUY internally inconsistent — validates `_remainingCarryForToken` (correct gate) then writes to vault. Tests codify the broken behavior. Fixing requires test updates too. **Game-design impact:** worker physically in Unicorn Town is irrelevant — only homebase vault is consulted. Bandit loot timing changes (vault drains immediately vs eventually). Bought goods are instantly upkeep-eligible without a return trip. |
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:5:**Spec references:** v4.2 §2.4, §7.7, §7.9-7.11, §8.2, §11.1, §15; dispatch brief `plans/drafts/20260426-phase3-dispatch-brief.md`
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:121:- Clan A in region R (homebase).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:122:- Clan B has a worker registered as defender at Clan A's base.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:133:**Setup:** Mint defender clan, send worker to target clan's base (`DefendBase(targetClanId)`). Travel duration = 1 tick.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:159:**Setup:** Register 3 workers (A, B, C) as defenders of same base. Confirm registry = [A, B, C].
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:185:**Action:** Advance several ticks. Let second bandit spawn and attack same base.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:187:**Assert:** W is still in `getActiveDefenders(A)` for the second attack. (v4.2 §8.2 — DefendBase is continuous.)
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:205:- Register N defenders at defender clan's base; their combined `defensePoints` > bandit's `attackPower`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:227:- Defender clan wall HP decremented.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:261:## 3.E5 — Loot split / wall damage / blueprint reward
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:279:### test_lootDistribution_blueprintAwardedToTopContributor
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:295:### test_wallDamage_onDefenderLoss
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:297:**Setup:** Defender clan with `wallHpCurrent = 100`. Bandit attack power exceeds defense.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:301:**Assert:** `Clan.wallHpCurrent < 100`. (Exact delta per implementation formula.)
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:324:- Register defender D at base of Clan A at tick T.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:332:## 3.E6 — Wall / base / monument upgrades
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:336:**Setup:** Clan starts at `wallTier = 0`, `wallHpCurrent = 0`. Clan has required stone in vault.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:341:- `Clan.wallTier == 1`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:342:- `Clan.wallHpCurrent == Clan.wallHpMax` (reset to max).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:348:**Setup:** Clan has `wallTier = 2`, `wallHpCurrent = 50` (damaged). Sufficient stone in vault.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:353:- `Clan.wallTier == 2` (unchanged).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:354:- `Clan.wallHpCurrent == Clan.wallHpMax`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:358:### test_upgradeBase_incrementsTier
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:360:**Setup:** Clan has `baseTier = 0`. Sufficient wood + stone.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:364:**Assert:** `Clan.baseTier == 1`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:370:**Setup:** Clan has `monumentTier = 0`. Sufficient gold + blueprints.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:374:**Assert:** `Clan.monumentTier == 1`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:378:### test_upgrade_rejectsInsufficientResources
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:386:### test_upgrade_costScalesWithTier
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:388:**Setup:** Upgrade wall from tier 0 → 1 (cost C0). Record cost. Upgrade from tier 1 → 2 (cost C1).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:390:**Assert:** `C1 > C0`. Confirms geometric cost scaling.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:396:### test_leaderboard_rankConsistentWithCompositeScore
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:398:**Setup:** Mint 3 clans with different gold, worker count, wallTier, monumentTier. Set known values so expected score order is deterministic.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:402:**Assert:** Returned clan order matches descending composite score:
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:404:score = vaultGold + (workers * N) + (wallTier * M) + (monumentTier * K) - banditDamageTaken
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:409:### test_leaderboard_rankUpdatesAfterBanditAttack
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:411:**Setup:** Clan A has higher score than Clan B. Bandit attacks Clan A (defender loss) — Clan A takes vault loss.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:415:**Assert:** Scores reflect vault/wall damage. Rank may have changed if Clan B now scores higher.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:427:### test_leaderboard_handlesMaxClans
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:446:- Clan A sends 2 defenders to Clan B's base.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:459:- Leaderboard reflects post-attack scores.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:489:| 3.E5 Loot / wall damage | 7 |

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8642:`_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget()` only clears defenders already registered in `_clansmanDefendingRegion`. Traveling `DefendBase` missions are not registered until arrival, and `_settleMissionForClansman()` does not re-check target liveness before `_registerDefender()`. A defender can therefore arrive later and become an active defender for a dead base. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `targetClanId == deadClanId`, or revalidate before registering on arrival.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8673:`_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget()` only clears defenders already registered in `_clansmanDefendingRegion`. Traveling `DefendBase` missions are not registered until arrival, and `_settleMissionForClansman()` does not re-check target liveness before `_registerDefender()`. A defender can therefore arrive later and become an active defender for a dead base. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `targetClanId == deadClanId`, or revalidate before registering on arrival.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8681:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).

docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7263:docs/planning/CANONICAL_SPEC.md:29:**The conflict:** `clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md §A7` originally stated "All v1 market actions are Exact Input actions only" and described `market_buy` as spending an exact amount of gold. This contradicts `IClanWorld.sol`, `clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md §8.3–8.4`, and `clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md`.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7385:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7389:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:77:The 3 single-model HIGHs from Codex 5.4 + 5.5 are not single-model = "low confidence." They're file-system level findings (ABI tuples, blueprint unit) that other models simply didn't grep at that depth. All 5 are real and should be addressed.

docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reference/prize-strategy.md:9:**Target:** ship a recordable demo of ClanWorld running inside the World App, end-to-end. The 8 demo moments from `docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v1_implementation_profile.md` §8.
docs/reference/prize-strategy.md:78:- **Same codebase**, parameterized by chain. Don't fork.
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:14:- One frontend codebase, parameterized by chain config at build/deploy time.
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:52:The Convex code stays in the codebase for two reasons: (1) disaster recovery, (2) self-contained CI / smoke tests with no external keeper.
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:76:  "chain": "base-sepolia" | "worldchain-sepolia",
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:117:## 8. No real funds in agent wallets
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:119:Agent (Elder) wallets are **hot-keys** holding only testnet faucet funds. Two-wallet model for S2:
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:120:- **Treasury wallet** — high-value ops, offline-signed.
docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md:121:- **Agent wallets** — hot, capped per-tick budget.
docs/reference/sponsor-tech.md:58:- **Open questions:** model availability, throughput, latency vs Anthropic baseline.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:3:These two rules override default "good engineering" instincts for the duration of the hackathon (Submission 1 — 2026-04-26, Submission 2 — 2026-05-05). Hackathon time is the bottleneck. After Submission 2 ships and the codebase has real users, these rules can be revisited.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:28:- **Property-based tests** unless the function is a pure data transform that's central to the demo.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:62:This codebase has no production users yet. Break env-var names freely when something better appears.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:66:- Each conceptual configuration value has exactly ONE name. If a value reaches both server and browser, the browser variant uses the Vite-required `VITE_` prefix on the SAME base name (e.g., `WORLD_APP_ID` server / `VITE_WORLD_APP_ID` browser). That is one concept, two transport names — fine. What's NOT fine is `WORLD_ID_APP_ID` AND `WORLD_APP_ID` both meaning "the World mini app's app id."
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:82:- ONE base name (e.g., `WORLD_APP_ID`).
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:104:# GOOD — one base name, with the Vite-required browser variant

docs/reviews/pr133-review-claude-opus.md:50:| 6 | `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` | 1222–1251 | **`scoreboardClans` computed as unmemoized IIFE every render.** Sorting + mapping snapshot rows runs on every parent re-render. **Fix:** Wrap in `useMemo` keyed on `snapshot`, `agentLogs`. | W1-A7, W2-A8 |
docs/reviews/pr133-review-claude-opus.md:52:| 8 | `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` | 1216–1221 | **Misleading comment about scoreboard when DEMO_MODE is off.** Comment says scoreboard hides when demo is off, but live snapshot data will populate it. **Fix:** Update comment to reflect actual behavior. | W1-A1, W2-A8 |
docs/reviews/pr133-review-claude-opus.md:59:| 15 | `apps/web/src/WorldMap.tsx` | 694–766 | **Async `Assets.load` callbacks lack mount guard.** Clan sigil/base asset loads resolve with `.then()` but only check `mounted`/`appRef` loosely. Fast unmount can still run `viewport.addChild(sprite)` after teardown. **Fix:** Add `if (cancelled) return` guard inside each `.then()` callback. | W2-A8 (new) |
docs/reviews/pr133-review-claude-opus.md:64:| 20 | `apps/web/tests/e2e/02-demo-mode.spec.ts` | 40–44, 62 | **Assertions coupled to literal `"IG"` substring.** Renames to Iron Guard mock data break tests without a real regression. **Fix:** Use `data-testid` on scoreboard entries. | W1-A5, W3-A11 |

docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10335:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10370:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:**Verdict: NEEDS_FIXES.** Phase 6 ships a working immediate + scheduled market with FIFO ordering, k-invariant-checked AMM, deterministic heartbeat ordering, and 116/116 green tests. But the resource-side accounting **inverts the spec**: §8.3-8.4 require SELL to source from worker carry and BUY to credit worker carry, while the implementation drains/credits the clan vault on both sides — and the BUY path is even internally contradictory (validates `_remainingCarryForToken` then writes to `_addToVault`). Two other meaningful concerns: the per-tick scheduled-queue cap was deleted in the fix-round leaving an O(n²) storage-sort with no upper bound, and the `MinimalERC20.mint/burn` "boundary" is dead code never invoked by the engine.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:### H1 — Market action source/destination violates spec §8.3 + §8.4 (carry vs vault)
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:13:Spec text (`docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md` §8.3-8.4):
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:24:The buy path is **internally contradictory** — it gates on worker carry capacity but mutates vault, so the carry-cap check no longer reflects what the action actually does. Practical consequences: a worker with full carry but an empty vault is rejected from buying; a worker physically present at UT with carry to sell is irrelevant — only the clan's homebase vault is consulted (vault is at the homebase, far from UT).
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:48:In `ClanWorld.t.sol`, the test computes `tick1 = world.getActiveMission(csId1).settlesAtTick` and `tick2 = world.getActiveMission(csId2).settlesAtTick` independently and walks `while (currentTick <= maxTick)`. There is no `assertEq(tick1, tick2, ...)`. If the two clans happen to have different baseRegion travel distances to UT, the failing buy and the OK sell land on different ticks, and the test passes by trivially proving cross-tick independence — not the same-tick FIFO-poisoning property the name implies. Add `assertEq(tick1, tick2)` (force the configuration if needed) and assert that both events fire in the same heartbeat.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:92:- **Scheduled enqueue + execute happen in the SAME heartbeat** (executeAtTick = settlesAtTick = arrivalTick + 1). This is a 1-tick delay vs the OLD `executeAtTick = arrivalTick`, but spec §11.3 only requires "is indexed under `executeAtTick` … executes eagerly at the corresponding heartbeat" — the new design is spec-compliant and conceptually cleaner (action duration is honored uniformly).
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:1:# PR #137 Review — merge: bundle E — Phase 2 economy (#91 post-rebase)
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:16:Previously reviewed in R1 and R2 rounds (fixes preserved in rebase): heartbeat try/catch, stale queued action invalidation, ceiling division overflow safety, market events with realized amounts, test naming.
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:66:| 15 | `src/ClanWorld.sol` | 1078–1103 | **Leading underscore on `external` functions.** `_executeMarketSellExternal` / `_executeMarketBuyExternal` use internal naming convention on external visibility. Justified by try/catch pattern but unconventional. Agent 7. | **DEFER** |
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:16:1. `clanworld_v4_spec.md` — base game mechanics
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:66:**Definition of done:** working demo recordable on a phone via World App, showing all 8 demo moments from `clanworld_v1_implementation_profile.md` §8.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:86:Per the user's confirmation: **the two realms are never both live simultaneously.** Submission 1 runs on World Chain Sepolia. After that submission ships, the same codebase is redeployed and reconfigured for Submission 2 on Base Sepolia.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:90:- **One frontend codebase**, parameterized by chain config at build/deploy time.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:120:| Frontend `tickEpoch.durationMs` (from Convex) | 20000 | 60000 | 20000 |
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:148:The code stays in the codebase per backend spec §6. It is feature-flagged off by default (`HEARTBEAT_CALLER_ENABLED=false` in `.env`). Two reasons it stays:
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:210:  "chain": "base-sepolia" | "worldchain-sepolia",
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:283:- **KeeperHub workflow.** Confirm the Base Sepolia workflow per v4.5 §2 is exactly what the bounty requires; check whether they want richer integration (e.g., conditional triggers based on game state).
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:323:- §2.13 (tick duration) → resolved per §2.1 of this doc
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:332:The graph applies to **one realm at a time.** Add a note: "This graph runs once for Submission 1 (World Chain Sepolia) and again for Submission 2 (Base Sepolia). Same codebase, different chain configs."
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:369:deploy-base-sepolia:
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:375:fund-agents:                     # uses CHAIN env to pick the right RPC and wallet set
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:380:Single env var (`CHAIN=worldchain-sepolia` or `CHAIN=base-sepolia`) drives all per-chain behavior.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:388:- **Two submissions, one stack, one realm at a time.** Same codebase, chain config swapped between submissions.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:435:| Wallets | One per agent, gas + game txs | **Two per clan** — agent wallet (gas, game txs, KV writes) + owner wallet (iNFT control); Submission 1 may use single wallet shortcut |
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:458:- **§4 Wallet Model** — wallet derivation from mnemonic, BIP-39 paths, funding, mapping file. Still correct.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:459:- **§8.2 Strategic alignment seeds** — Aldric/Mira/Brennan/Sora archetypes are pure prompt content. Still useful for both submissions; just paste them into CLAUDE.md instead of injecting via `system_prompt`.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:486:- Pumps situation blocks based on the same triggers as 0G battleplan §9 (waiting clansmen, new whisper, mission completion, tick advanced + 60s heartbeat)
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:489:- Reads game state from Convex; submits transactions via the Elder wallet
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:491:The single-wallet shortcut (battleplan §9) is acceptable for Submission 1.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:511:- Two-wallet model: agent wallet + owner wallet, never co-located
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:518:- Submission 2 adds: Town Square panel (3 slots × N clans, reads from 0G KV bulletins via Convex mirror), Owner Editor UI (separate route, wallet-connected, edits iNFT metadata), Transfer Demo flow (the killer Track 2 moment)
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:549:4. **The orchestrator spec** — full version with situation pump, AXL bridge, DEK reading, multi-chain event listening, two-wallet model
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:593:**What:** a wallet-connected page where the human owner edits the persistent strategy & notes tail of their clan's CLAUDE.md, signs the change with their owner wallet, and triggers an `updateMetadata()` tx on 0G iNFT contract.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:595:**Why:** the Track 2 prize narrative includes "dynamic upgrades" — owners updating their agent's intelligence post-mint. Without this UI, the dynamic-upgrade story is hand-waved. With it, judges see a real demo.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:601:**Dependencies:** ClanIdentity library (read-write mode), wallet connector, iNFT contract deployed.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:606:1. Owner of clan 7 transfers iNFT to a new wallet
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:610:5. The new Elder's first action references something specific from the prior owner's gameplay (e.g., "based on my notes, clan 3 betrayed us at tick 412, do not trust them")
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:628:- **Two submissions, one stack, one realm at a time.** Same codebase, chain config swapped between submissions.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:629:- **Submission 1 (World) is first.** World Chain Sepolia, 20s ticks, Foundry-loop heartbeat, MiniKit-wrapped frontend, simple Claude Code Elders with minimal `elder` toolbelt, single wallet per agent, Convex `whispers` table.
docs/planning/V1/07 clanworld_v4_5_alignment_addendum.md:630:- **Submission 2 (Base + OpenAgents Track 2 + others) is later.** Base Sepolia, 60s ticks, KeeperHub heartbeat. Plus: 0G iNFT (ERC-7857 with MockOracle) for Elder identity, 0G KV for agent memory + town square bulletins, AXL for private whispers, two-wallet model, Owner Editor UI, Transfer Demo. Plus possibly: 0G Compute sealed inference (model selection TBD).
