docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8299:  Dead-target cleanup only releases defenders that have already arrived and been registered in `_clansmanDefendingRegion`. A clansman still traveling on a `DefendBase` mission toward a clan that dies keeps the mission active, can arrive later, and gets registered to defend a base that no longer exists. The added cleanup test settles the defender into `ACTING` before killing the target, so this path is still uncovered.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8307:  The camp-duration fix has a positive boundary test, but no matching “one tick too early must fail” assertion. That leaves the off-by-one boundary only partially pinned.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8335:  Dead-target cleanup only releases defenders that have already arrived and been registered in `_clansmanDefendingRegion`. A clansman still traveling on a `DefendBase` mission toward a clan that dies keeps the mission active, can arrive later, and gets registered to defend a base that no longer exists. The added cleanup test settles the defender into `ACTING` before killing the target, so this path is still uncovered.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8343:  The camp-duration fix has a positive boundary test, but no matching “one tick too early must fail” assertion. That leaves the off-by-one boundary only partially pinned.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:21:### M3. `_markClanDead` swallows the `reason` string parameter
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:25:`ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830`: the victim pool is the entire `_clanClansmanIds[clanId]` filtered only by `state != DEAD`. A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when a bandit attacks the home base. This may be intentional ("base raid causes generalized casualties"), but it's a notable design choice that the spec extract in `docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md` should confirm. If not intentional, restrict the pool to `cs.currentRegion == clan.baseRegion` (or `cs.state == ClansmanState.WAITING`/`DefendBase`).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:27:### M5. `_applyBanditWallDamage` absorbs a flat 100 HP regardless of wall level
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:28:`ClanWorld.sol:~1772-1788`: `wallDamage = remainingDamage < WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL ? remainingDamage : WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL;` followed by `if (wallDamage >= WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL) clan.wallLevel--`. So a level-5 wall absorbs the same 100 damage as a level-1 wall before being chipped, and any single attack that lands ≥100 damage chips one level. Higher-level walls give no extra incoming-damage cushion per attack — they only give *more attacks* before the wall is fully gone. Verify this matches the v4.5 spec; if the intent was "level N wall absorbs N×100", the math is off.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:31:`ClanWorld.sol:~1716-1717` calls `_settleClan(targetClanId)` and `_eagerSettleActiveDefendersForBase(targetClanId, …)` on every attack resolution, but step 3 (`_eagerSettleForBandits`) already settles candidate-region bases and defenders on the same closedTick. With the typical 4-clansman x 12-clan limit this is bounded, but it's pure wasted gas under the live-attack path. Once H1 is fixed and attacks actually fire, this redundancy will compound. Consider removing the redundant settle inside `_resolveBanditAttack` — the heartbeat-level eager-settle already guarantees the target is current.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:34:`ClanWorld.sol:~547-572`: bounded by 12×4=48, so safe today, but if `MAX_CLANS` ever grows the iteration count goes quadratic in the global clansman population. Cheaper alternative: walk `_defendingClansByRegion[baseRegion]` and only inspect that clan's clansmen. Defer-OK while caps stay tight, but worth a TODO.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:45:`_tickSeeds[0]` and `_tickSeeds[1]` are both zero at deploy time (no constructor seed). Spawning is gated by `MIN_SPAWN_COOLDOWN_TICKS`, so first spawns can't happen at tick 0–1 anyway, and mission RNG mixes in `(clansmanId, nonce, tick)` which still gives per-mission distinct hashes — but the deterministic-from-zero base is technically attackable by anyone who can pre-image `keccak256(abi.encode(…, 0x0, …))`. Trivial mitigation: seed `_world.currentTickSeed = keccak256(abi.encode(block.prevrandao, address(this)))` in the constructor.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:464:VERDICT: **NEEDS_FIXES**. While the Phase 9 PR successfully integrates foundational spawn chance logic and deterministic attack resolution, it suffers from critical architectural drift regarding the bandit state machine and eager-settlement scope. Bandits lack automated transitions to select targets or recover from escapes, rendering the threat entirely inert in practice. Additionally, wall defense calculations and view-only settlement simulations contain edge cases that undermine base progression and UI accuracy. 
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:475:Sub-issue 9.3 mandates eager-settling bases and defenders "before target selection" to ensure targets are chosen based on fresh, up-to-date loot values. However, `_eagerSettleForBandits()` includes an early return `if (_activeBanditCount >= MAX_TOTAL_BANDITS) return;`, completely bypassing all settlement when the global bandit cap is reached. Furthermore, it only settles regions that pass `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate()`—ignoring regions that have existing `Camped` bandits waiting to select targets but aren't currently eligible for a *new* spawn. This guarantees stale target evaluation.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:483:*Suggested fix:* Update the logic to allow the wall to absorb up to `clan.wallLevel * WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` total damage. Calculate the number of wall levels lost based on the actual damage absorbed, and deduct multiple levels if the damage exceeds a single level's HP threshold.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:485:**Isolated view-only simulation creates zombie defenders for dead bases**
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:488:*Suggested fix:* In `_simulateSettleMissionForClansman` (or `getDerivedClansmanState`), perform a quick read of the target clan's storage state (`_clans[m.targetClanId].clanState`). If the target base is `DEAD`, safely mock the clansman's state as `WAITING` with an inactive mission.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:12:This document is the source of truth for the ClanWorld demo client. It specifies what the user sees, how the codebase is organized, and exactly what data the client subscribes to from Convex.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:31:2. **Drama over fidelity.** A cheap pulse-and-flash on a bandit-threatened base beats a perfectly-rendered combat animation. Make the moments unmissable.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:47:| `gold-bright` | `#D4A24C` | Highlights, leaderboard winner |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:79:Clan color appears on: clansman caps, base flags, leaderboard rows, mission log entries, speech bubble borders.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:96:- Building (base/wall/monument): 16×16
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:103:- Buildings: 48×48 base, 32×64 monument tower (taller as level rises), 32×16 wall segment
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:154:- Building sprites (base, wall, monument) at each homebase
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:169:- Columns: rank, clan banner color + name, monument level (visual stack of stones), wallLevel/baseLevel chips, lootValue, alive/dead state
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:170:- Sorted by monument level desc, then earliest-tick-reached, then lootValue (matches v4 §9.1)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:185:- Tapped a base: shows clan stats, vault contents, walls, monument, defenders
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:193:| Clan spawn | World: new sprite walks onto map. HUD: leaderboard adds row. Ticker: `Cobalt clan has founded their homebase at East Farms` |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:195:| Resources become useful | World: wheelbarrow fill animation, deposit animation at base. Inspector: vault counter ticks up |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:197:| Bandits threaten | World: bandit camp sprite, red pulse on projected target base, attack countdown chip in HUD |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:199:| Meaningful upgrade | World: building sprite animates one tier up. Leaderboard: re-sorts |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:200:| Scoreboard pressure | Always-on: top HUD season/tick, leaderboard panel |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:213:- Camera zoom range: 0.5× to 1.5× of base scale
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:214:- Atlas: `atlas_world.png` (8×8 clansmen, 16×16 buildings)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:371:  tickEpoch: { tick: number; atMs: number; durationMs: number };
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:376:`tickEpoch` answers "what wall-clock time was the most recent tick advance?" It comes from the Convex `worldSnapshot.currentTick` field plus a `tickAdvancedAt` timestamp Convex records. The duration is the configured tick duration (15–20 seconds for the demo).
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:382:The contract has only 4 states: `WAITING | TRAVELING | ACTING | DEAD`. The UI splits `ACTING` into 5 visual statuses based on the active mission's action. The derivation function applies this mapping first, then computes position.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:411:  ticksElapsed = currentTick - mission.startTick + (now - tickEpoch.atMs) / tickEpoch.durationMs
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:431:- For DEFENDING, anchor near the defended base position with a small jitter offset.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:510:- `world_base_lvl1..5` — base building strategic icon
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:511:- `world_wall_lvl1..5` — wall decoration around base
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:512:- `world_monument_lvl0..10` — monument tower at strategic scale
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:528:- `base_lvl1..5_<color>` — building, color reflects clan banner
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:529:- `wall_lvl1..5_<color>` — wall segments
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:530:- `monument_lvl0..10` — monument tower
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:536:- `effect_pixel_burst` — 4 frames (used for trades, deposits, blueprint awards)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:546:- Source text comes from `agentLogs` Convex query. The agent runner sets `triggeringClansmanId` on each log; if set, the bubble appears over that clansman. If unset (e.g., for `do_nothing` or `send_whisper` decisions), the bubble appears over the clan flag at the homebase. See agent runner spec §7.1 for the attribution rules.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:551:- World view: 40 strategic sprites + 8 buildings + 8 region accents + maybe 1 bandit ≈ 60 sprites, all static
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:552:- Detail view: ~40 animated sprites + buildings + ambient motion ≈ 80 sprites with animation
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:619:| Leaderboard tab | `getSnapshot` | tick advance, building events |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:635:  | { kind: 'building_upgraded'; tick: number; clanId: number; building: 'wall' | 'base' | 'monument'; newLevel: number; createdAt: number }
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:638:  | { kind: 'blueprint_awarded'; tick: number; clanId: number; amount: number; createdAt: number }
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:641:  | { kind: 'otc_transfer'; tick: number; fromClanId: number; toClanId: number; gold?: number; blueprint?: number; resources?: ResourceCarry; createdAt: number }
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:732:      buildingSprite.ts
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:793:- Travel interpolation visibly works (sprite walks between two regions over the configured tick duration)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:834:- Tick advance timestamp handling: when does Convex consider a tick to have advanced? On the `TickAdvanced` event being indexed, or on a wall-clock heuristic?
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:840:- When does an agent's reasoning get attributed to a specific clansman vs. the clan as a whole? Clan-level reasoning still shows as a speech bubble — over which clansman? **Locked: the first clansman in the missions array.** If `do_nothing` or `send_whisper` is called (no missions array), the bubble appears over the clan flag at the homebase.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:856:- Account auth / wallet connection (demo runs with hardcoded agents)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:880:2. World canvas renders all 8 regions with terrain and clan homebases
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:885:7. Leaderboard reorders when monuments level up

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10601:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10611:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:551` — target-death cleanup misses en-route defenders. `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget` requires `_clansmanDefendingRegion[clansmanId] == baseRegion`, but traveling defenders are not registered until arrival, so their `DefendBase` mission can survive a target death and later register against a dead clan. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `mission.targetClanId == deadClanId`, registered or not.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10643:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10653:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:551` — target-death cleanup misses en-route defenders. `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget` requires `_clansmanDefendingRegion[clansmanId] == baseRegion`, but traveling defenders are not registered until arrival, so their `DefendBase` mission can survive a target death and later register against a dead clan. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `mission.targetClanId == deadClanId`, registered or not.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:60:- one wallet (derived from the team mnemonic)
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:88:**LLM provider choice.** Anthropic's Claude (Sonnet) is the primary. Reasons: tool-use is reliable, structured outputs are clean, latency is acceptable at 15–20s tick cadence, and the `claude-haiku` fallback exists for cost-conscious mass play. OpenAI's GPT-4o is a fine fallback. Both via the same internal `LLMClient` interface so we can swap.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:125:  // Sonnet at hackathon time. Haiku-tier is the cost-conscious fallback.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:141:  agentThinkBudgetMs: 12000,                  // hard cap on LLM call duration
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:158:All agent wallets derive from one BIP-39 mnemonic via standard Ethereum HD paths:
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:225:**Why the jitter:** four agents simultaneously calling the LLM on the exact same wall-clock tick produces correlated rate-limit issues and crowds the Anthropic API. Spreading by 0–2s smooths the load and feels more natural in the UI when reasoning bubbles appear.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:328:              targetClanId: { type: 'number', description: 'Required for DefendBase. The clanId of the base being defended.' },

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:420:You command a small clan of clansmen across an 8-region world. Your goal is to outlast and outbuild rival clans by building the tallest monument and surviving until season end.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:427:- Be decisive. Long deliberation costs ticks.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:443:| Aldric | "Fortify and endure. Build walls early, hoard wood, defend trades. Never strike first, but punish those who strike you." |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:445:| Brennan | "Strike for monument. Race the tower. Take risks, take losses, but build before the others do. Bandits are someone else's problem." |
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:461:Base lvl {baseLevel}, Wall lvl {wallLevel}, Monument lvl {monumentLevel}
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:462:Vault: wood {w}, iron {i}, wheat {wh}, fish {f}, gold {g}, blueprints {bp}
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:473:- Clan {id} ({color}): monument {ml}, base {bl}, wall {wl}, livingClansmen {n}, lootValue {lv} {dead ? "[DEAD]" : ""}

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:806:The runner is **stateless across restarts**. On boot it reads the mapping file, derives wallets, and starts polling Convex. No on-disk state to corrupt.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:860:- What's the gas estimate for `submitClanOrders` with 5 missions? Need to make sure agent wallets are funded enough for 360 ticks × multiple agents.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:31:**Impact:** A bandit could spawn into a region where bases and defenders were NOT eager-settled, meaning the very next attack resolution sees stale clan state. This is a correctness bug, not a security bug — the clan will settle lazily later, but attack damage calculations against stale vaults/defender counts could be wrong.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:69:wallDamage = remainingDamage < WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL ? remainingDamage : WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL;
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:70:remainingDamage -= wallDamage;
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:71:if (wallDamage >= WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL) {
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:72:    if (clan.wallLevel > 0) {
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:73:        clan.wallLevel--;
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:78:A wall with level 5 absorbs at most `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) damage and drops exactly one level. This means a bandit with strength 500 against a level-5 wall will: absorb 100 via wall (drop to level 4), then pass 400 through to base defense and clansmen. The wall only provides a single level of protection per attack regardless of wall level. This may be intentional game design, but it means wall levels 2-5 only provide cosmetic insurance (multiple attacks needed to fully destroy the wall). Worth confirming this is the intended behavior since the wall cost scales significantly with level.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:122:5. **Cross-clan defense (`_validateDefendBaseOrder` change) is a significant gameplay expansion.** Any clan can now defend any other clan's base by specifying `targetClanId`. The validation correctly checks that the target clan exists and is alive, and that `gotoRegion` matches the target's `baseRegion`. This is well-integrated with the loot distribution system (`_activeDefendingClanIds` scans all defending clans in the region).
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md:20:Every other backend concern is out of scope for the hackathon. No auth, no rate limiting, no admin UI, no analytics, no replay, no pagination. We're building a 6-hour demo, not a service.

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md:388:The contract's `heartbeat()` is permissionless. In production, anyone can call it on any cadence ≥ `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS`. For a demo, we don't want to depend on someone else calling it, and we want demo-friendly tick durations (15–20s, not 60s).

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md:456:The caller wallet is a standard derivation from the team mnemonic (the Makefile handles funding). Each successful heartbeat costs gas. On Base, this is fractions of a cent per call. Pre-fund with $1 of ETH and forget.

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:141:Backend spec §11 questions whether the per-clan filter on `agents.getRecentLogs` is actually used. Adding it requires an index, which adds storage cost.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:149:**Recommended:** keep the index. Inspector tab benefits, cost is trivial.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:153:Agent runner observation rendering (spec §8.3) consumes `recent` as chronologically ordered (oldest → newest, so trend direction is "left to right increasing").
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:161:Backend spec §8 says no auth on `agents.postLog` and `whispers.post`. Agents are trusted in v1.

docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:16:- **0G ERC-7857 documentation:** [docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/erc7857](https://docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/erc7857)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:17:- **0G INFT integration guide:** [docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/integration](https://docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/integration)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:18:- **0G INFT overview:** [docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/inft-overview](https://docs.0g.ai/developer-hub/building-on-0g/inft/inft-overview)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:35:The repo uses Hardhat + pnpm. Solidity 52% / TypeScript 48% by content. The deployment uses an upgradeable proxy pattern — community deployment guides reference both `AgentNFTImpl` and `TEEVerifier` deployed addresses, which means the `AgentNFT` is the proxy and `AgentNFTImpl` is the implementation. We don't need to care about the upgradeability for hackathon — just follow the reference deployment flow.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:65:| Faucet | `https://faucet.0g.ai` (0.1 0G/day/wallet — request more in Discord if needed) |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:73:Per battle plan §10: **deploy on mainnet** (Aristotle). The 0.1 OG/day testnet faucet limit is going to be annoying with 4 elders running heartbeats, GLM-5 Compute calls if used, KV writes, and iNFT mint+update transactions. A small mainnet OG balance (a few OG covers the entire demo lifecycle — see Gas costs in §7) is cleaner than rationing testnet drips.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:75:For the iNFT specifically, since we deploy `MockOracle` (which skips real TEE re-encryption on transfer), the prize narrative is the same on mainnet vs testnet — both demonstrate the same `MockOracle`-based contrived transfer with manual DEK handoff. Use mainnet to match the rest of the deployment.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:93:- Held by the owner locally (UI / wallet / file)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:146:Includes the URI in the event payload, so the watch handler has everything it needs in the event itself. Trivial size cost in event logs.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:150:**Answer: `eth-crypto`** ([npmjs.com/package/eth-crypto](https://www.npmjs.com/package/eth-crypto)). It supports ECIES encrypt/decrypt for secp256k1 keys, which is what wallet keys are. Maintained by Daniel Meyer; widely used; standard pick.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:167:Note: ECIES uses the wallet's secp256k1 keypair directly. `EthCrypto.publicKeyByPrivateKey(privateKey)` derives the pubkey if you only have the privkey. Make sure to use the **uncompressed pubkey** (without the `0x04` prefix per `eth-crypto`'s convention).
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:278:- **Agent wallet** — separate wallet with minimal funds, used by the orchestrator for game txs (battle plan §9). Never holds the iNFT.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:280:For demo simplicity: contract deployer == iNFT owner (one wallet does both). Agent wallet is separate. Owner UI runs with the iNFT-owner wallet. Orchestrator runs with the agent wallet.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:309:- An owner wallet with at least 1 OG on mainnet (sufficient for several deploys + dozens of clan mints/edits at hackathon scale)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:375:pnpm hardhat clan-bind --network base-mainnet \
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:392:- **Replaced on transfer** — for the contrived MockOracle-based demo transfer, the new owner's UI manually receives the DEK from the old owner (out-of-band as part of the demo script) and writes it to their own clan-{tokenId}-dek.hex. Real TEE flow would re-key automatically; we don't.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:411:- A `MockOracle`-based contrived transfer (rubber-stamped proof + manual DEK handoff between owner UIs) is enough to demonstrate the *intelligence-travels-with-iNFT* narrative for the pitch — see battle plan demo punch line
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:416:What we *do* demonstrate end-to-end: the iNFT contract's storage shape, the owner-driven `updateMetadata()` path, the encrypted blob format, the cross-wallet boot path, the agent picking up its KV memory after ownership change. These are real and verifiable. The TEE oracle's verify-and-re-encrypt step is the only thing stubbed.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:418:### Gas costs
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:429:Total for 4 clans + a handful of strategy edits during the demo: well under 1 OG worth of gas. The compute costs (GLM-5 if used, 0G Storage uploads) will dominate any blockchain gas costs by several orders of magnitude.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:436:- **Owner UI implementation** — frontend forms, wallet connection logic, file export. Out of scope here.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:438:- **Transfer flow** — the real TEE re-encryption flow is out of scope for hackathon (no real TEE oracle deployed). The `MockOracle`-based contrived transfer used in the demo is described in §7 ("MockOracle vs real TEE oracle"). For the genuine flow if implementing later, see EIP-7857 and the reference impl's `transfer()` function.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:469:- ❓ Whether `AgentNFT` proxy is a UUPS or Transparent proxy — affects how upgrades work post-deployment. We don't plan to upgrade during hackathon, so deferred.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_inft_deployment_notes.md:479:- ❌ Multi-deployer / governance / admin keys beyond a single deployer wallet
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:36:- "iNFT-minted agents with embedded intelligence (encrypted on 0G Storage), persistent memory, dynamic upgrades"
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:48:| Dynamic upgrades | Owner edits "persistent strategy & notes" tail of CLAUDE.md via UI, signs with wallet, updates iNFT metadata; agent freely evolves its KV notebook |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:55:"Watch what happens when I transfer this clan iNFT to a new wallet. The new owner's Elder boots up, reads its CLAUDE.md and skills from the encrypted iNFT metadata, and on its first turn reads its own notebook in 0G Storage KV — including the entry from tick 412 about clan 3's betrayal. The new owner inherits not just identity but lived experience."
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:92:        │                        │                       │ wallet tx
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:127:| Game state (vault, missions, world tick, etc.) | Base/World chain | Deterministic, settleable, prize ranking |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:130:| Persistent strategy & notes (tail of CLAUDE.md) | 0G Storage (encrypted, ref'd by iNFT) | Owner-edited via UI, signed wallet tx |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:138:| Owner→Elder strategy updates | Owner UI → wallet tx → re-encrypt iNFT | Durable, edits CLAUDE.md tail section |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:155:5. **Build the Elder loop** (Claude Code session + orchestrator + toolbelt). One Elder first, then four. See §5–§8.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:187:1. Same wallet on both chains (default for EVM addresses)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:283:Agent **does not** write to this. The persistent strategy section is owner-controlled — edited via the owner UI, signed with the owner's wallet, and committed to the iNFT as a re-encrypted blob update. The agent reads it as part of its CLAUDE.md system prompt.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:338:| claudeMd (including persistent strategy tail) | ✅ via owner UI + wallet tx | ❌ |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:339:| skills | ✅ via owner UI + wallet tx | ❌ |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:340:| personality | ✅ via owner UI + wallet tx | ❌ |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:343:**The agent never writes to this blob.** All agent-driven state (observations, plans, grudges, learnings) lives in 0G Storage KV under the agent's clan namespace. The KV namespace is bound to clanId and is wallet-gated — when the iNFT transfers, the new wallet inherits write access to that KV namespace, and so the agent's full notebook travels along.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:347:Earlier drafts had a structured `reputation` field in the encrypted blob that the agent could update via `elder rep`. This was complicated: it required the agent to call `commit` explicitly to re-encrypt the blob, it constrained the agent to a fixed schema (trust scores, notes), and it duplicated functionality that KV already provides cleanly.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:349:New model: agents record their understanding of other clans in KV notebook with whatever schema they prefer. Since KV is bound to clanId and gated by wallet ownership of the iNFT, the notebook transfers with the iNFT just like the encrypted blob does — without needing a separate re-encryption step. Simpler, more flexible, more "agent-native."
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:354:- Owner saves changes to the persistent strategy tail (or skills/personality) via UI — owner signs a wallet tx that triggers re-encryption + on-chain hash update via `updateMetadata()`. The DEK is unchanged; only IV and ciphertext change.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:365:**Bound to clanId. Access control TBD pending 0G Storage KV semantics check (§11 #9).** The intended model: the wallet that owns the corresponding ERC-7857 iNFT has authoritative write access to this namespace, so when the iNFT transfers, write access transfers with it — agent's notebook travels with ownership without any separate copy step.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:370:2. **Orchestrator-mediated gating** — if 0G Storage KV is open-write or uses a different model, the orchestrator validates iNFT ownership before signing each write on behalf of the agent wallet. The agent wallet is registered as the namespace's authorized writer in some side-mapping, and gets rotated on iNFT transfer. More plumbing, but works regardless of KV's native auth.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:424:For hackathon: polling cadence is fine. Webhook-based push is a polish item, not required.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:453:**Strategic / persistent** — owner edits the "Persistent Strategy & Notes" tail section of CLAUDE.md via a dedicated UI form (separate from the chat box), signs with their wallet, and the iNFT blob is re-encrypted with the new content. Durable. Travels with the iNFT on transfer. This is the channel for high-level direction shifts ("we're switching from peaceful trader to warlord") and long-lived guidance ("never trust clan 6").
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:474:**Two distinct wallets per clan, never co-located:**
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:478:| Agent wallet (`AGENT_WALLET_KEY`) | Claude Code session env | Game txs (`elder act`), KV signed writes, AXL identity | Minimal — only enough gas for ongoing gameplay |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:479:| Owner wallet (`OWNER_WALLET_KEY`) | Owner UI / desktop wallet, **never** in agent env | iNFT mint, iNFT metadata edits (persistent strategy / skills / personality), iNFT transfer, prize claims | Real funds — owner's primary wallet |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:481:**Why split.** The agent reads untrusted input from other clans (bulletins, whispers) and could be socially engineered or prompt-injected. Claude Code agents can run arbitrary bash, which means anything in their env is reachable. If the owner's main wallet were in the agent's env, a successful prompt injection could drain it directly via ethers, bypassing the toolbelt's namespace restrictions entirely. With split keys, the worst case is the agent makes bad in-game decisions or empties its own gas wallet — both recoverable, neither catastrophic.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:483:**Authorization on Base/World contract.** The clan's `Clan.owner` field holds the agent wallet address, not the owner wallet. The owner wallet retains control via the iNFT on 0G — when ownership transfers, the new human owner generates a fresh agent wallet for the inherited clan and updates `Clan.owner` accordingly. (Mechanism: an owner-only `setClanAgent(clanId, newAgentAddress)` function on the Base/World contract, gated by checking iNFT ownership on 0G. For hackathon, can be admin-gated as a simplification.)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:485:**Note for hackathon scope.** This split adds some plumbing complexity. If we run out of time, an acceptable shortcut for the *demo only* is to use a single low-funded wallet that serves both roles, with the owner UI just signing iNFT updates from the same key. Don't deploy this pattern with real funds.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:522:    - 14 (homebase, off cooldown, carrying wood=15)
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:593:> Hi — building a multi-agent game for the OpenAgents hackathon, Track 2. A few questions:
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:597:> 3. Does 0G Storage KV support per-namespace authorized-writer gating tied to a signing key? We want to gate writes to a `clan:{N}:*` namespace based on the wallet that owns a corresponding ERC-7857 iNFT.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:608:| 4 | Reset cadence (5–25 ticks) | Tuning | Step 5 | Start at 10, adjust based on demo behavior |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:609:| 5 | Whether to ship persistent strategy editor UI mid-game | Scope | Step 6 | Default: hardcode at mint; cut priority #3. Supporting infrastructure exists in `ClanIdentity` (read-write mode + `updatePersistentStrategy`) and the contract (`updateMetadata` per deployment notes §3 TBD #3) — what's missing is the frontend form and wallet-signing integration |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:645:- ✅ Agent state lives in 0G Storage KV under `clan:{clanId}:*`, namespace bound to clanId, wallet-gated, transfers with iNFT
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:651:- ✅ Owner→Elder strategy updates go through dedicated UI form (separate from chat); signed wallet tx; updates iNFT
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:653:- ✅ Orchestrator pumps `<situation>` blocks; agents are long-running not turn-based
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:679:- **Clan** — the game entity owned by a player. Has clansmen (workers), a base, a vault.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:47:  retryConfig: { maxAttempts: 3, baseDelayMs: 200 },
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:59:| `signer` | `ethers.Signer` | yes | Agent wallet signer; used by auth strategy for signing writes; library never inspects the underlying key |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:64:| `retryConfig` | `{ maxAttempts: number; baseDelayMs: number }` | no | Defaults to `{ maxAttempts: 3, baseDelayMs: 200 }` |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:72:- Lets the caller use hardware wallets, KMS, or other signing backends if desired
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:91:The signer is the **agent wallet**, not the owner wallet (per battle plan §9). It's used to sign 0G KV writes via the auth strategy and to authenticate as the agent identity.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:136:Sugar for `(await read(key)) !== null`. Same network cost as `read`. Use only for readability when the value isn't needed.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:216:Per battle plan §7 and §8, the recipient's own orchestrator writes to its inbox after polling AXL. The orchestrator's ClanMemory instance is configured with the recipient's `clanId`, so writes to `inbox:{N}:*` are within-namespace from its perspective. A different clan's orchestrator can never write here because its instance has a different `clanId` lock.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:284:The library exposes thin convenience helpers for these well-known patterns (see §8) but never as a *replacement* for raw `read`/`write`. Conveniences are sugar; the raw API is the contract.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:357:If 0G KV supports per-namespace authorized-writer gating with a registered signing key, this strategy signs each write with the agent wallet key directly. Cleanest, no orchestrator involvement.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:498:Exponential backoff on transport errors, configurable via `retryConfig`. Default: 3 attempts, base delay 200ms, exponential factor 2 (so 200ms, 400ms, 800ms).
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_memory_spec.md:553:Hit live 0G mainnet KV with a throwaway agent wallet. Tests:
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:71:await identity.updatePersistentStrategy("Build coalition; fortify base 0007.");
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:91:| `ownerSigner` | `ethers.Signer` | yes | read-write | Owner's wallet signer; signs `updateMetadata()` txs; library checks `ownerOf(tokenId)` matches the signer's address at construction |
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:96:Both modes need the DEK; the distinction is whether the caller has the owner's wallet signer:
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:98:- **Read-only mode** — the orchestrator runs with the agent wallet (per battle plan §9), which is **not** the iNFT owner. It has no authority to call `updateMetadata()`. It just needs to decrypt and serve up CLAUDE.md, skills, etc. to the Elder session.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:99:- **Read-write mode** — the owner UI runs with the owner wallet. It can both decrypt and submit on-chain updates.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:144:3. **Sealing layer (real TEE flow only, not exercised in our deploy)** — during a real ERC-7857 ownership transfer, the TEE oracle decrypts the blob, generates a new DEK, re-encrypts the blob, and seals the new DEK with the receiver's secp256k1 public key via ECIES. The sealed DEK is published in the transfer event so the receiver can unseal it with their wallet's private key. Our `MockOracle` deploy skips all of this — the contrived demo transfer just hands over the existing DEK out-of-band.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:181:- New owner receives sealed DEK via transfer event payload, unseals with their wallet, saves to their own local file
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:267:The orchestrator runs with the agent wallet, but the DEK belongs to the iNFT owner. We need to get the DEK from the owner's machine into the orchestrator's environment without requiring the owner's private key to be present at orchestrator runtime. Three flows are possible; we pick the simplest for hackathon.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:301:- **Transfer support** — when a clan iNFT transfers, the new owner would need to repeat the file flow with their own machine. Real ERC-7857 mechanics: TEE oracle seals the new DEK to their pubkey during transfer, they unseal with their wallet, save to their own local file. Our deploy uses `MockOracle` which doesn't perform the seal/unseal — the contrived demo transfer instead hands over the existing DEK manually between owner UIs as part of the demo script. The library doesn't help with either flow; it just gets reconstructed against whatever DEK file the new owner has.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:462:Thrown when the loaded blob's `version` field is not supported by the current library version. Indicates an upgrade is needed.
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:551:Hit live 0G mainnet with a throwaway wallet. Tests:
docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_clan_identity_spec.md:588:- **Owner UI implementation** — frontend forms, wallet connection, file write of DEK. Separate workstream.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:227:All functions are `view`. Readers and indexer pay no gas. The only cost is bytecode size for the additional structs and functions, which should be negligible.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:241:With v1's max realm size of 8 clans, unbounded array returns are safe. If a future version grows beyond ~50 clans, add pagination to `getWorldSnapshot.leaderboard`.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4679:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 7 ships: gold transfer + vault transfer + blueprint transfer + bundled transfer + dead-clan restrict. Atomic-swap pattern (proposer + acceptor, NOT order book per Liam directive). Builds on Phase 5/6/10.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4706:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10611:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:551` — target-death cleanup misses en-route defenders. `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget` requires `_clansmanDefendingRegion[clansmanId] == baseRegion`, but traveling defenders are not registered until arrival, so their `DefendBase` mission can survive a target death and later register against a dead clan. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `mission.targetClanId == deadClanId`, registered or not.
docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4707:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10653:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:551` — target-death cleanup misses en-route defenders. `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget` requires `_clansmanDefendingRegion[clansmanId] == baseRegion`, but traveling defenders are not registered until arrival, so their `DefendBase` mission can survive a target death and later register against a dead clan. Clear all active `DefendBase` missions with `mission.targetClanId == deadClanId`, registered or not.
docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4761:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 7 ships: gold transfer + vault transfer + blueprint transfer + bundled transfer + dead-clan restrict. Atomic-swap pattern (proposer + acceptor, NOT order book per Liam directive). Builds on Phase 5/6/10.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4801:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:546:- Source text comes from `agentLogs` Convex query. The agent runner sets `triggeringClansmanId` on each log; if set, the bubble appears over that clansman. If unset (e.g., for `do_nothing` or `send_whisper` decisions), the bubble appears over the clan flag at the homebase. See agent runner spec §7.1 for the attribution rules.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5093:The spec slice is loaded: Phase 7 only promised the transfer surface plus dead-clan blocking on initiation, while the deeper state-schema doc still describes direct transfer entrypoints. docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:16:- World logic remains tick-based; slight real-time drift does not change rules semantics.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:34:## A3. Bandit movement duration between regions
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:55:- target selection is not based only on the eventually selected base
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:56:- target selection is computed only after all candidate bases in the current region have been eagerly settled
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:92:- if a base begins a tick with `0` wood in vault,
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:101:- carried resources do not save a base from upkeep until they are actually deposited
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:157:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:175:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:184:- 10 defense via `defend_base`, or
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:185:- 5 defense via `WAITING` at their own homebase
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:33:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1899-1906` (`_applyBanditWallDamage`) | 4.6 + 4.7 + Gemini = **3/5** | MED | **Wall absorbs flat 100 HP regardless of level.** Level-5 wall absorbs same 100 damage as Level-1. Higher walls only give more attacks before destruction, not more cushion per attack. Verify v4.5 spec intent — if "level N wall absorbs N×100", the math is off. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:36:| M4 | `ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830` (`_pickBanditClansmanVictim`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **Victim pool too broad.** A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when bandit attacks home base. Confirm intent vs. restrict pool to base-region defenders. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:64:- **Codex 5.4:** NEEDS_FIXES — 4 HIGH (incl. ABI staleness + winter replay + blueprint unit) + multiple LOW
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:75:- Single-model findings: **rest** — including 3 of the 5 HIGH (winter replay, blueprint unit, attack-revert-on-death)
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:77:The 3 single-model HIGHs from Codex 5.4 + 5.5 are not single-model = "low confidence." They're file-system level findings (ABI tuples, blueprint unit) that other models simply didn't grep at that depth. All 5 are real and should be addressed.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:14:- region-based movement instead of fine-grained tile pathfinding
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:97:## 1.8 Homebase placement
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:98:Each clan has one homebase assigned to a valid spawn region.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:183:If a mission is submitted during tick `307` with travel duration `1`:

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:238:The cooldown is **time-based**, not tick-count based.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:284:- deposit resources, then wait at Homebase

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:325:## 3.11 `defend_base` persistence
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:326:`defend_base` is a **persistent continuous mission**.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:333:A clansman on `defend_base` remains on that mission until:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:398:Each homebase has an effectively infinite vault.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:405:- homebase upgrades
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:406:- monument upgrades
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417:- base yield = `2e18` wood per action tick
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:423:- base yield = `0.5e18` iron per action tick
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:459:- regrow duration is `4` ticks
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:471:These costs apply only if the clan has living clansmen.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:492:- do not force heartbeat-based full clan settlement solely to flip starvation state
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:572:If multiple scheduled market actions mature in the same tick, Keeper executes them in deterministic FIFO order based on mission commit order.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:684:- baiting or shifting target priority toward another base
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:690:- identify the highest loot-value homebase in the current region
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:691:- if no base exists in the region, attack resolution is a noop
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:692:- if multiple bases are tied for the highest loot-value, including a zero-loot tie, target the base with the lowest `clanId`
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:828:## 7.2 Winter duration

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:859:- every `2 coldDamage` reduces `wallLevel` by `1`
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:860:- once `wallLevel == 0`, every `2 additional coldDamage` kills `1 clansman`
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:862:Clansman deaths from cold apply only while wall level is zero.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:877:The following are **single-tick actions** that consume one full action tick at homebase:

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:885:All building actions consume resources from the homebase vault, not from carried worker inventory.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:985:A season does **not** end immediately when a clan first reaches monument level 10.

docs/planning/CANONICAL_SPEC.md:29:**The conflict:** `clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md §A7` originally stated "All v1 market actions are Exact Input actions only" and described `market_buy` as spending an exact amount of gold. This contradicts `IClanWorld.sol`, `clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md §8.3–8.4`, and `clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md`.
docs/planning/CANONICAL_SPEC.md:54:`apps/landing/src/pages/LorePage.tsx` contains gameplay constants (gather durations, cooldown descriptions) written for narrative clarity. These are not authoritative. Where they diverge from rank 1–8 docs, rank 1–8 wins.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:17:Senior staff engineer FINAL pre-merge review for PR #199 (Phase 8 — Buildings + Progression) at head 33193b7.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 8 ships: wall upgrades (8.1) + base upgrades (8.2) + monument upgrades w/ blueprint gating L7-10 (8.3) + score / rank getters (8.4). Builds on Phase 5 vault + Phase 9 blueprint reward.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:832:+        returns (uint256 score, uint64 monumentReachedAtTick, uint8 monumentLevel)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:837:+    /// @notice Return live clan rankings sorted by score descending, with clanId ascending for exact ties.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:874:+        scores = new uint256[](liveCount);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:877:+            scores[i] = tempScores[i];
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:889:+        returns (uint256 score, uint64 monumentReachedAtTick, uint8 monumentLevel)

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:894:+            monumentReachedAtTick = _monumentLevelReachedAt[clanId][monumentLevel];

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1883:+    function _expectedScore(uint8 level, uint64 reachedAtTick, uint256 lootValue, uint8 wallLevel)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1898:+        return (uint256(level) << 248) | (timeComponent << 184) | (lootValue << 8) | wallLevel;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1917:+        (uint256 scoreA, uint64 reachedA, uint8 levelA) = world.getClanScore(clanA);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1918:+        (uint256 scoreB, uint64 reachedB, uint8 levelB) = world.getClanScore(clanB);

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1928:+        assertEq(scoreB, _expectedScore(levelB, reachedB, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanB)), "B score");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1929:+        assertEq(scoreC, _expectedScore(levelC, reachedC, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanC)), "C score");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1930:+        assertGt(scoreA, scoreB, "higher monument level wins");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1931:+        assertGt(scoreB, scoreC, "earlier reach tick wins");

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1941:+        assertEq(scores[2], scoreC, "rank 3 score");

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2419:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-synthesis.md:18:**Single-model new HIGH (Codex 5.4 only):** `_pickBanditAttackTarget` ranks competing targets from RAW unsettled clan state. Eager-settle only covers spawn-candidate regions, skipped at global bandit cap. Multi-clan target selection can pick the wrong clan based on stale loot/liveness in production.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:28:`ClanWorld.sol:~1772-1788`: `wallDamage = remainingDamage < WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL ? remainingDamage : WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL;` followed by `if (wallDamage >= WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL) clan.wallLevel--`. So a level-5 wall absorbs the same 100 damage as a level-1 wall before being chipped, and any single attack that lands ≥100 damage chips one level. Higher-level walls give no extra incoming-damage cushion per attack — they only give *more attacks* before the wall is fully gone. Verify this matches the v4.5 spec; if the intent was "level N wall absorbs N×100", the math is off.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3688:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:31:`ClanWorld.sol:~1716-1717` calls `_settleClan(targetClanId)` and `_eagerSettleActiveDefendersForBase(targetClanId, …)` on every attack resolution, but step 3 (`_eagerSettleForBandits`) already settles candidate-region bases and defenders on the same closedTick. With the typical 4-clansman x 12-clan limit this is bounded, but it's pure wasted gas under the live-attack path. Once H1 is fixed and attacks actually fire, this redundancy will compound. Consider removing the redundant settle inside `_resolveBanditAttack` — the heartbeat-level eager-settle already guarantees the target is current.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3689:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3695:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:72:    if (clan.wallLevel > 0) {
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3696:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:73:        clan.wallLevel--;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3703:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3704:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:483:*Suggested fix:* Update the logic to allow the wall to absorb up to `clan.wallLevel * WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` total damage. Calculate the number of wall levels lost based on the actual damage absorbed, and deduct multiple levels if the damage exceeds a single level's HP threshold.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3705:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3711:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3712:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3716:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3966:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7812:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:290:        emit BlueprintEarned(clanId, banditId, world.blueprintUnit(), world.getWorldState().currentTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3998:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4497:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
